examples.title: Basic GLUT Menu Creation
examples.description: Simple example demonstrating creation and attachment of a basic GLUT menu
examples.explanation: This code creates a basic pop-up menu attached to the right mouse button. When any menu item is selected, the menuCallback function receives the associated integer value and executes appropriate action using a switch statement.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for menu operations, O(n) where n is number of menu items in callback
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for menu system plus O(n) for callback switch handling

examples.title: Hierarchical Menu with Submenus
examples.description: Example demonstrating creation of cascading submenus in GLUT
examples.explanation: This demonstrates hierarchical menu creation. Submenus must be created BEFORE they are added to parent menus using glutAddSubMenu(). The callback handles selections from all levels by checking different value ranges.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for menu creation, O(n) callback where n = total menu items
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k = number of menus (main + submenus)

examples.title: Dynamic Menu Management
examples.description: Example showing how to destroy and recreate menus dynamically during runtime
examples.explanation: This demonstrates dynamic menu management: detaching a menu from a button, destroying it to free resources, creating a new menu, and reattaching. Useful for context-sensitive menus that change based on application state.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = menu items being created/destroyed
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for new menu allocation, O(1) for cleanup
